controls.value: Longmont Public Library Launches Annual Summer Reading Challenge

Longmont’s summer reading challenge is underway, running from May 21 to July 31. This year, the Longmont Public Library has organized various events and activities tailored for children aged infant through fifth grade. The challenge aims to keep young minds engaged during the summer break while fostering a love of reading.

Librarians will host yoga storytime on Thursday, July 2 from 10 am to 10:30 am [Source](evidence:47). Participants can enjoy stories, movement, and mindfulness activities that promote early literacy skills. On the same day, there will be a Spanish-English conversation group for language learners practicing conversations in either language [Source](evidence:47).

For those interested in gaming, the library offers a free gaming club on Wednesdays from 6 pm to 7 pm [Source](evidence:47). Kids aged third through fifth grade can play Nintendo Switch games provided by the library or bring their own.

The Longmont Youth Center will host several summer programs throughout July. On Thursday, July 2, from 1 pm to 5:30 pm, kids can participate in the Navigators program [Source](evidence:47), which offers a range of activities. The Glam Group and Make it Better programs also run during this time [Sources](evidence:47) [Sources](evidence:47).

The challenge encourages children to log their reading hours, with incentives for reaching various milestones. Participation is open to all local youth, fostering community engagement and educational support.

For more information on registration processes and expected outcomes, residents are advised to check the library’s calendar of events or contact the Longmont Public Library directly [Source](evidence:49).
